column2: Meaning: to learn about something by reading
column3: Example: I need to read up on the phrasal verbs before my next lesson.
column4: Tags: 6 phrasal verbs about reading; British Council

column2: Meaning: to check something by reading it again
column3: Example: Can you read over my email before I send it?
column4: Tags: 6 phrasal verbs about reading; British Council

column2: Meaning: to read from the beginning to end
column3: Example: She read through the contract very carefully.
column4: Tags: 6 phrasal verbs about reading; British Council

column2: Meaning: to turn pages quickly without reading in detail
column3: Example: She flipped through a magazine at the doctor's.
column4: Tags: 6 phrasal verbs about reading; British Council

column3: Example: The teacher read out the story to the class.
column4: Tags: 6 phrasal verbs about reading; British Council

column2: Meaning: to read something very carefully
column3: Example: She pored over the map to find the best route.
column4: Tags: 6 phrasal verbs about reading; British Council
